title: Conjecture 2 (Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood)
context_text: Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood conjectured that the defective chromatic number of the class of $H$-minor-free graphs equals $\mathrm{td}(H)-1$ for every graph $H$. The lower bound $\chi_{\Delta}(\mathcal{M}_H) \geq \mathrm{td}(H)-1$ was already established; the upper bound was known only in special cases such as $H = K_t$ and complete bipartite graphs.
attributed_to: Ossona de Mendez, Oum, and Wood
paper_contribution: Provides the first evidence for the upper bound direction by proving that $\chi^*(\mathcal{M}_H)$ — and hence $\chi_{\Delta}(\mathcal{M}_H)$ — is bounded above by a function of $\mathrm{td}(H)$ (specifically $\chi^*(\mathcal{M}_H) \leq 2^{\mathrm{td}(H)+1} - 4$), which was previously unknown.
notes: PDF source — math notation partially garbled; statement reconstructed from context. The conjecture header reads 'Conjecture 2 ([15])'.

title: Conjecture 4
context_text: Theorem 3 establishes the matching lower bound $\chi^*(\mathcal{M}_{H_k}) \geq 2k-2$ for graphs $H_k$ with $\mathrm{td}(H_k) = k$, showing that defective and clustered chromatic numbers can differ. The authors conjecture this lower bound is tight, and note the conjecture is proved in Section 4 for the special case $\mathrm{td}(H) = 3$.
notes: PDF source — math notation partially garbled; statement reconstructed from context. Introduced with the phrase 'We conjecture an analogous upper bound'.
